name: Foldovers
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald milk and stir in sugar and salt until cool to lukewarm.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. Stir the cooled milk mixture into the yeast mixture.
4. Add flour and mix well. Turn dough onto a floured board and knead until elastic (about 5 minutes).
5. Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled (about an hour).
6. Brown hamburger, onion, and celery in oil; stir to make the meat crumbly.
7. Remove excess fat from the mixture.
8. Stir in remaining ingredients and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
9. When dough has doubled, roll out very thin on a floured board. Cut into evenly sized squares.
10. Divide beef mixture into six equal portions and place one portion in the middle of each dough square.
11. Fold over to form turnovers and seal edges with a fork.
12. Place foldovers on a lightly oiled baking sheet, cover, and let rise for 30 minutes in a warm place.
13. Make a small slit in the top of each foldover.
14. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes until cooled.
15. Spread butter on each cooled foldover and wrap tightly in foil.
16. For future use, freeze wrapped foldovers before the picnic.
17. At the picnic, place wrapped foldovers on a grate and heat. Thawed foldovers take about 5 minutes per side; frozen foldovers require close to 20 minutes per side.
